title: Improve rootless setup experience for arch based systems
body:
/kind feature

**Description**
To get podman running on a newly setup manjaro system you need to follow the following instructions:
https://wiki.archlinux.org/index.php/Linux_Containers#Enable_support_to_run_unprivileged_containers_(optional)

Maybe this could all be wrapped into an (AUR?) package (e.g. named something like podman-rootless), so it is easier to find for people new to this level of details regarding containers.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.2.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.1
Built:              Tue Apr  2 20:01:51 2019
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```


comments.body: We would need you or someone else to volunteer to maintain the AUR package, if you want to udpate the install.md for information on arch, please open a PR.

> We would need you or someone else to volunteer to maintain the AUR package

Yes, that was also my idea.
I have no experience in this topic and I'm asking myself if this will be something that is still in flux a lot and could easily get out of hands regarding maintenance effort. (Changes could come from arch, kernel, podman, ...?)

If it is likely that such a package it will break on a regular basis I'm quite sure I'll **not** be able to maintain it on my own in addition to getting used to the whole packaging part.

Any opinions?

Well I have no experience in packaging on Arch, no experience in Arch at all.  Podman releases probably ever 6 weeks or so now, so you would need to do some work when this happens.  (Potentially more if CVE's happen).  But the turn should not be that much.

We would obviously help out on Podman issues that arise.   I would doubt the Kernel or other parts of Arch would cause much problems.  You might be able to ask other Arch packagers to help out and get a second maintainer.

OK, I don't know any other arch packagers ...
It sounds doable but I hope creating this issue with the idea in it will attract somebody with more knowledge and some time to pick it up.
Currently it is not a very high prio for me, sorry.
You can decide if it is worth keeping this issue open for the purpose of this or if it is a better idea to close it.
